definition: to leave someone or something behind without intending to return
example: Never abandon a pet.

definition: present in large quantities; more than enough
example: The forest has abundant wildlife.

definition: to increase speed; to cause something to happen sooner
example: The car began to accelerate on the highway.

definition: to accept or recognize that something is true
example: Acknowledge your mistakes so you can improve.

definition: to publicly support a cause; a person who supports a cause
example: She advocates for better school lunches.

definition: to state something confidently as true
example: Affirm your goals every morning.

definition: behaving in a forceful or hostile way
example: An aggressive player may receive a penalty.

definition: to assign resources for a specific purpose
example: Allocate enough time for studying.

definition: to change something slightly
example: The tailor altered the dress to fit better.

definition: having more than one possible meaning; unclear
example: The instructions were ambiguous and confusing.

definition: to expect or predict something will happen
example: I anticipate a great performance tonight.

definition: clearly visible or obvious
example: It was apparent that she was tired.

definition: to evaluate or judge the quality of something
example: The teacher will assess your writing.

definition: to give someone a task or role
example: The teacher assigned a book report.

definition: a quality belonging to someone; to credit something to a cause
example: Kindness is an admirable attribute.

definition: very strange or unusual
example: The artist painted a bizarre picture.

definition: to work together with others on a project
example: Scientists collaborate to find new cures.

definition: to give something to make up for a loss
example: They compensated us for the delay.

definition: having the skills and knowledge to do something well
example: She is a competent and reliable worker.

definition: to understand something fully
example: I could not comprehend the complicated text.

definition: to reach an agreement by both sides giving something up
example: We reached a compromise on the plan.

definition: to form an idea in the mind; to become pregnant
example: The engineer conceived a brilliant invention.

definition: to reach a decision after thinking or after finishing something
example: What can we conclude from this data?

definition: to say strongly that something is wrong
example: The judge condemned the unfair treatment.

definition: to face someone or something directly
example: It is brave to confront your fears.

definition: aware of and responding to your surroundings
example: Be conscious of how your words affect others.

definition: to give permission; agreement
example: You need parental consent for the trip.

definition: a result or effect of an action
example: Staying up late has consequences.

definition: always behaving or performing in the same way
example: Be consistent in your practice.

definition: to make up or form something
example: Twelve months constitute one year.

definition: to build or put something together
example: They will construct a new library.

definition: to think about something carefully for a long time
example: I contemplated my decision for days.

definition: to say the opposite of what someone else said
example: Your answer contradicts the evidence.

definition: causing disagreement or debate
example: The new rule was controversial among students.

definition: to match or be similar; to communicate by writing
example: The two stories do not correspond.

definition: the standards used to judge or evaluate something
example: What are the criteria for the award?

definition: extremely important; critical
example: Getting enough sleep is crucial for learning.

definition: to reach a conclusion based on evidence
example: From the clues, I deduced the answer.

definition: done on purpose; careful and unhurried
example: It was a deliberate and thoughtful choice.

definition: to show or represent something in a picture or description
example: The painting depicts a winter landscape.

definition: to get or obtain something from a source
example: Many English words derive from Latin.

definition: to discover or notice something
example: The alarm can detect smoke.

definition: to move away from the expected path or standard
example: Do not deviate from the plan.

definition: to become or make smaller or less
example: The noise gradually diminished.

definition: to treat people unfairly based on differences
example: It is wrong to discriminate against others.

definition: a disagreement or argument
example: They had a dispute over the rules.

definition: to change something so it is no longer accurate
example: Stress can distort your thinking.

definition: showing great variety; very different from each other
example: Our class is diverse and multicultural.

definition: very detailed or complicated; to give more detail
example: Please elaborate on your answer.

definition: to make sure rules or laws are followed
example: Teachers enforce classroom rules.

definition: to improve or make better
example: Music can enhance your mood.

definition: to make sure something happens
example: Please ensure that the door is locked.

definition: clearly seen or understood; obvious
example: Her talent was evident from the start.

definition: to develop gradually over time
example: Languages evolve and change over centuries.

definition: to deliberately leave someone or something out
example: Do not exclude anyone from the game.

definition: to display something publicly; a public display
example: The museum will exhibit new paintings.

definition: stated clearly and in detail
example: Follow the explicit instructions carefully.

definition: to reveal something hidden; to leave unprotected
example: The article exposed the truth.

definition: covering a large area or range
example: She has extensive knowledge of history.

definition: to make something easier to happen
example: Technology facilitates communication.

definition: to create or develop something in a detailed way
example: Scientists formulate hypotheses before experiments.

definition: forming the most basic and essential part of something
example: Respect is fundamental to any friendship.

definition: to produce or create something
example: The wind turbines generate clean energy.

definition: to suggest something without saying it directly
example: Her silence implied she was upset.

definition: to include something as part of a whole
example: Incorporate vegetables into every meal.

definition: to conclude something from evidence or reasoning
example: From the clues, we can infer the answer.

definition: the ability to act without being told to; a plan
example: Take initiative and start the project.

definition: to combine parts into a whole; to include all people equally
example: Integrate art into your daily life.

definition: to explain the meaning of something
example: How do you interpret this poem?

definition: to carefully examine and research something
example: Scientists investigate causes of diseases.

definition: based on clear reasoning
example: His explanation was logical and easy to follow.

definition: to make small changes to improve something
example: Modify your essay to fix the grammar.

definition: not influenced by personal feelings; a goal
example: Try to stay objective when making decisions.

definition: a point of view; a way of seeing things
example: Consider different perspectives before deciding.

definition: exact and accurate
example: Give a precise measurement in centimeters.

definition: a basic rule or belief
example: Honesty is an important principle.
